markdownRemark.excerpt: EMPLOYMENT HISTORY 06/2019 – Present Expedia Group (Lodging Shopping) - Brisbane, QLD (https://www.expedia.com) Engineering Manager Managed team of 6 engineers…
markdownRemark.html:
EMPLOYMENT HISTORY

06/2019 – Present
Expedia Group (Lodging Shopping) - Brisbane, QLD (https://www.expedia.com)
Engineering Manager

  • Managed team of 6 engineers, across a larger distributed team working in a squad-based model.
  • Part of a global team working across 6 locations in North America, Australia, India and UK, serving Expedia’s hotel shopping experience across a portfolio of brands including Expedia, Orbitz, Travelocity, Wotif, amongst others.
  • Technical contribution towards both the front end (React/Typescript), backend (Kotlin/Java/Spring Boot), and Android (Kotlin/Java/Rx) codebases.
  • Bug fixing and live site support.

08/2018 – 11/2018
Expedia Group (Trover) - Brisbane, QLD (https://www.expediagroup.com)
Snr Software Engineer (Android) https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.trover

  • Internal 3 month rotation into Trover team to lead the development effort to update and modernise the Trover app (travel photo sharing), which had been in maintenance mode for 3 years focusing on an increase in community engagement.
  • Part of a skeleton development team working remotely from 5 locations across the USA and Australia (4 time zones).
  • Moved average Play Store review score from 3.75 to 4.3 stars in the space of 3 months.
  • Lead prioritisation and development to update internal architecture and dependencies - unblocking the ability for the team to release the app to the Play Store.
  • Lead the redesign of app main screen and navigation using modern UI paradigms and toolkits.
  • Piecemeal conversion of 2012 era Java codebase to Kotlin.
  • Android Studio (IntelliJ) based development. Gradle build scripting.
  • UX design/prototyping using Sketch.
  • Installed CI/CD pipeline into release flow using CircleCI.

11/2014 – 06/2019
Expedia Group (Partner Central) - Brisbane, QLD (https://www.expediagroup.com)
Snr Software Engineer (Android): 03/2018 - Present
Software Engineer II (Android): 11/2014 - 03/2018
https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.expediapartnercentral

  • Lead Android developer for the Partner Central hotelier extranet app, leveraging a close working relationship with product managers and the UX team.
  • Mentoring of junior developers, including regular 1:1s.
  • Modern Android development using Kotlin/Java and Android Architecture Components.
  • Hybrid app development including use of both Android native, and web views.
  • Lead the initiative of using a custom token authentication mechanism across both Android and iOS, including secure storage of user credentials for the Android platform.
  • Test driven development (TDD) against a suite of unit and integration tests.
  • Instrumental in the setup and configuration of CI/CD pipeline using Jetbrains TeamCity.
  • Cross platform development of some screens using React Native. Later conversion to vanilla ReactJS (web).
  • Android Studio (IntelliJ) based development. Gradle build scripting.

02/2013 – 10/2014
Wahoo Fitness - Atlanta, GA (http://www.wahoofitness.com)
Android Developer (Lead) https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.wahoofitness.fitness

  • Lead the development effort to port the Wahoo Fitness workout tracker app from iOS to Android while inheriting partially implemented code base.
  • Managed team of 3 external contractors in US while working out of Brisbane, Australia.
  • Modern fragment-based UI development following Android design guidelines (targeting API 16+).
  • Bluetooth Smart (Low Energy) development to communicate with Bluetooth GATT devices.
  • Implemented authentication and sharing to web services using OAuth2.
  • Both Eclipse and Android Studio (IntelliJ) development. Gradle build scripting.
  • Distributed source control using Git.
  • Release management to Google Play production and beta channels.
  • Graphic design for application art assets.

11/2011 – 10/2012
Self Employed - Coorparoo, QLD
Lead Developer

  • Development of custom game engine from scratch in C++ using OpenGL and Bullet Physics libraries.
  • Cross platform development using SDL.
  • Use of best practice design techniques and patterns including platform independent renderer (using Visitor pattern), entity component architectures (Composite patterns).
  • Designed and implemented own scene graph.
  • Designed and implemented own generic animation subsystem.
  • Use of 3D maths – vectors, matrices, quaternions, trigonometry.
  • Implemented own build system (using makefiles) that detects and builds across multiple platforms (OS X, mingw, and Ubuntu environments).
  • Host development on Mac OS X. Initial development on Ubuntu (8.04 to 10.04).
  • Source control using Git. Both local and remote repository administration.
  • Mediawiki setup and administration on Ubuntu 10.04.

10/2009 – 09/2011
Invensys Rail – Eight Mile Plains, QLD
Software Engineer

  • Member of software team in onboard systems group developing second-generation Automatic Train Protection system. Product written in Ada, test simulator in Delphi.
  • Ported prototype application scheduling program from Windows to Ubuntu Linux environment using pthreads, POSIX semaphores and some x86 assembly. Some exposure to VxWorks.
  • Primary maintainer of build system (makefiles).
  • Wrote many tools for auditing purposes (code counting, coverage testing) in Java SE.
  • Performed bug fixing/investigation on an unfamiliar software system during last phases of development under intense pressure to release.
  • Administered automated test system in preparation for formal validation.
  • Build manager – IBM Telelogic Synergy. Created release builds for software in high pressure periods, including many late nights. Wrote numerous scripts to query change control system to create reports for team leader.
  • Maintained CruiseControl projects to keep automated build system working.
  • Mediawiki setup and administration.

08/2006 – 10/2009
Invensys Rail – Eight Mile Plains, QLD
Software Engineer

  • Key member of core team implementing and maintaining main SCADA control centre product “SystematICS”, targeted to control centres for the railway industry, used across multiple business units. Product written for UNIX/Linux almost entirely in C++, with some parts written in C.
  • Stood in for team leader when absent, both long term (weeks), and short term.
  • Implemented HTTP-based subscription protocol client (C++).
  • Maintained TCP/IP connection handling code.
  • Designed and implemented Java server (using Apache Tomcat) for HTTP subscription protocol.
  • Documentation duties including User Requirements, Software Requirements, Software Module Design Specifications, and developer API tutorials.
  • Build/release management responsibilities using IBM Telelogic Synergy source code management tool.
  • Performed critical build responsibilities at software release time.
  • Prepared reports for software release notes.
  • VBA development for Microsoft Access based SCADA system configuration application.

06/2005 – 08/2006
App-Tek International – Brendale, QLD
Firmware Engineer

  • Design and implementation of firmware for company’s flagship product as part of a small team.
  • Embedded C development on MSP430 platform using Rowley Associates CrossWorks compiler and IDE.
  • Design of structured modular firmware product with clear interfaces between software components.
  • Formal documentation of firmware modules including traceability to system requirements.
  • Full source code documentation using Doxygen documentation generation software.
  • Concurrent development using Subversion (SVN) source control.

03/2004 – 08/2006
App-Tek International – Brendale, QLD
Systems Development Engineer

  • Development and deployment of custom online document control system across R&D, QA, and Production departments in C#/ASP.NET (web-based intranet). Development in Visual Studio .NET 2003.
  • Development of online Request for Change system for R&D department (including task assignment and notification functionality).
  • Bridging of existing parts/product database to document control system to provide online access to released part drawings for use in production department.
  • Upgraded existing MS Access based parts/product database to SQL Server.
  • Extended functionality of existing parts/product database to allow for full Bill of Materials structure/breakdown.

03/2003 – 06/2003
Creative Audio (Now Biamp) – Windsor, QLD
Student Engineer (university industry placement)

  • Developed a graphical configuration application for the next generation of public address hardware.
  • Development in Visual Studio .NET (C++) using Microsoft Foundation Class Library.

PROJECTS

Oct 2018 - Present
Wireless cage monitor

  • Cloud connected hardware to monitor pet cage status/health (door status and location).
  • Android Things monitoring software (Kotlin) deployed on Raspberry Pi hardware.
  • Android client app (Kotlin) for display of status and receiving push notifications.
  • Firebase Cloud Function (TypeScript) to listen for monitor updates, and generation of FCM push notifications when necessary.
  • Solar powered battery and waterproof case for fully autonomous operation.

Dec 2014 - Present
Multi-function monitor for agriculture - “FarmHand”

  • Android app to monitor hardware inputs from tractor/implements (speed, area, bin and tank levels).
  • External sensor monitoring using IOIO-based hardware (https://github.com/ytai/ioio).
  • Background session processing using Android services.
  • Session/job management.
  • Alarm management: low/high alarms, snoozing.
  • Support for various hardware configurations.

Oct 2011 - Present (See employment above, Nov 2011 – Oct 2012)
Game / 3D Engine - “Run”

  • Side scrolling shooter.
  • 2 dimensional gameplay using 3D world/engine.
  • Run and gun with vehicles.

Dec 2010 - Present
Particle engine - “ParticleArt”

  • Dynamic art creation application using simple shapes (points, lines, bezier curves), particle emitters, and emitter boundaries.
  • Cross-platform architecture (Java: Android/Desktop).
  • Developed with OpenGL ES 2.0 (using vertex/fragment shaders, point sprites).

EDUCATION

1999 - 2003:

  • Griffith University - Bachelor of Microelectronic Engineering/Bachelor of Information Technology.
  • Graduated from dual degree. Computer Systems (Eng), Computer Science (IT).
  • GPA 5.14 (Scale 1-7, 7 being highest).

1994 - 1998:

  • Ingham State High School – completed year 12.
  • Overall position (OP) of 3 (Scale 1-25, 1 being highest).
  • Awarded subject prize for Physics.

COMPETENCIES

Professional Experience

  • Proficient in Kotlin/Java, Android framework, C/C++, Unix shell scripting (bash, ksh, regex), C#, ASP.NET, XML/XSLT, Visual Basic, SQL.
  • Moderate experience in Embedded C, Ada, Delphi/Pascal, MS Office Automation.
  • Some experience in Objective-C/Cocoa Touch (porting from iOS to Android), x86 Assembly, Perl.
  • Proficient with Linux/UNIX (Ubuntu/Debian, Mac OS X/BSD, Solaris, Redhat), Microsoft Windows and command line.
  • Proficient with GCC toolchain + vi, Android Studio/IntelliJ IDEA, Visual Studio Code, and others.
  • Source code control using Git, Subversion, IBM/Telelogic Synergy.

Non-professional Experience (Hobbyist)

  • OpenGL, Objective-C/Cocoa, PS2 Linux (EE and GS programming), JavaScript, MIPS Assembly.
  • Demo application available on request.

INTERESTS

Music: songwriting, guitar, band (https://www.facebook.com/leavenmusic, http://leaven.bandcamp.com)
Computers: user interface design, computer graphics, game development, computer architecture, computer history.
Other (misc): architecture, story/scriptwriting, film/video production, electronics, cars (body work, engines).

frontmatter.title: Farmhand: A multi-function monitor for precision agriculture
